relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: YHWH calls from the completed tent of meeting.
The people are taught how to bring-near an offering.
A complete herd offering is brought to the entrance for acceptance.
The hand rests on the head, joining the giver to the gift.
Blood/life is brought to the altar by the priests.
The offering is opened and prepared.
Fire and wood are arranged by priestly hands.
The pieces are ordered upon the altar.
Washed inward parts and legs rise as a soothing scent.
A complete male from flock may also be brought near.
Blood/life is thrown around the altar.
The flock offering is arranged upon the fire.
Washed and wholly offered, it rises to YHWH.
Bird offerings make nearness possible for smaller means.
The priest brings the bird to the altar.
Waste is removed to the place of ashes.
The bird rises on the fire as a soothing scent.

contemplative_note: Leviticus begins not with distance but with a call: the Divine dwelling now teaches the people how to draw near with whole-hearted offering.
